# SpielGantt Project Agent Instructions

This folder is a SpielGantt project. Treat the ordinary project folder as the
durable artifact and use SpielGantt metadata only where it is already defined.

Use the project-local skills in `.agents/skills`:

- `use-spielgantt` for CLI syntax, JSON contracts, validation, and mutation
  rules.
- `setup-spielgantt` when the user wants to turn a scientific workflow plan
  into tasks, events, dependencies, event endpoints, and task statuses.
- `update-spielgantt` when the user wants to revise an existing SpielGantt
  project.
- `review-spielgantt` when the user wants read-only project review, validation
  findings, repair reports, exports, or proposed repairs before applying any
  changes.

Preserve user files outside `.spielgantt/`. Structured SpielGantt metadata is
canonical; shortcuts, links, and generated summaries are never the source of
truth.

## SpielGantt Boundary

`spielgantt` is CLI-first. Rust/core is the package-domain library behind that
CLI, and package inspection or mutation should use CLI JSON contracts and CLI
commands. GUI features may call the same Rust behavior directly, but package
semantics must remain CLI-expressible.

GUI/session and desktop-only behavior is not package truth. Visual layout,
timeline row/lane placement, connector drawing, transient interaction state,
remembered-project UI state, file watching, and whole-project deletion are GUI
concerns, not Rust/core package commands. Dependency validity, legal dependency
choices, event references, validation, file-format behavior, and package
mutation rules are package semantics owned by Rust/core and exposed through the
CLI.
